content: Just as language learners consolidate new vocabulary and correct usage errors through deliberate, repeated reflection on their writing practice in high English curricula, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be improved by explicitly framing iterative self-correction as a structured learning exercise that mirrors the scaffolded reflection activities used in human education. This framing suggests adding spaced repetition of reflection cycles for common error types, similar to how students revisit reflection on vocabulary use across multiple writing assignments to build long-term accuracy.
